phrases.french: J'ai une réservation pour la visite des caves.
phrases.english: I have a reservation for the cellar tour.
phrases.phonetic: zhay ewn ray-zair-vah-SYON poor lah vee-ZEET day KAV
phrases.context: Veuve Clicquot's historic cellars in Reims span 24km of tunnels. The house was founded in 1772 and became famous under the Widow (Veuve) Clicquot's leadership.

phrases.french: La célèbre étiquette jaune, c'est la marque de fabrique ?
phrases.english: The famous yellow label — that's the trademark?
phrases.phonetic: lah say-LEB-ruh ay-tee-KET ZHON, say lah mark duh fah-BREEK
phrases.context: The Yellow Label (étiquette jaune) non-vintage is Veuve Clicquot's signature. Madame Clicquot also invented the riddling rack (pupitre) for clarifying champagne.

vocabulary.english: widow (the brand name means 'The Widow Clicquot')

tips: Booking is essential — Veuve Clicquot tours are among the most popular in Reims
Madame Clicquot took over the house at 27 after her husband died and invented the riddling process still used today
The La Grande Dame cuvée is named in her honour — worth asking about in the tasting room
